meta.title: Offshore Safety Case — Argument Map (Thistle Bravo, worked example)
meta.subtitle: Fixed production platform — SI 2015/398 Schedule 6/7 claim → argument → evidence
meta.author: offshore-safety-case (assistive)
meta.classification: Confidential — competent-person review required; precedes competent-authority acceptance
meta.deid_notice: All personnel are referred to by role label. Identifiers were pseudonymised before drafting; the re-identification key is held separately and is not part of this document.

sections.text: This worked example structures the duty-holder's offshore safety case for the Thistle Bravo fixed production platform under SI 2015/398 (SCR 2015) as a goal-structured claim → argument → evidence map. Where the duty-holder supplied evidence it is recorded with its provenance; where it was not supplied the element is recorded [GAP]. No QRA or consequence figure is computed or invented here, no barrier/SCE is asserted effective without a cited performance standard, and the output does not assert acceptance — it precedes competent-authority acceptance.
Top claim (to be demonstrated by the duty-holder's evidence): major-accident hazards on Thistle Bravo are controlled so that risks to personnel and the environment are reduced to ALARP.

rows: Identify MAH from the duty-holder's HAZID/bowtie
Prevention + detection + mitigation barriers, each to a performance standard
Record the external QRA with provenance (never computed here)
List the SCEs and the performance standard each must meet
Record the verifier findings against the SCE performance standards
sections.caption: Goal-structured safety-case argument — element / strategy / evidence / status. Unsupplied evidence is [GAP], never asserted Supported.

items: HAZID-TB-2025 and bowtie analyses BT-01 to BT-04 (supplied)
Fire & gas SCE performance standard PS-07 with verifier finding (supplied)
External QRA / consequence-modelling report — [GAP], not yet supplied (the skill records, never computes)
SCE register TB-SCE-v3 — two performance standards still [GAP]
Emergency response (EER) — see the marine-emergency-response (MAR-03) plan for Thistle Bravo

rows.title: External QRA / ALARP demonstration not supplied
rows.description: The ALARP demonstration cannot be assembled without the duty-holder's external QRA. Recorded [GAP] — no figure is invented or computed.

rows.title: Two SCE performance standards outstanding
rows.description: Two SCEs in register TB-SCE-v3 lack a stated performance standard; neither is asserted effective until the standard and a verifier finding are supplied. Recorded [GAP].

rows.control: Inherently safer design — topsides inventory minimisation
Fire & gas detection with automatic deluge (SCE PS-07, verified)

rows.action: Supply the external QRA / consequence analysis so the ALARP demonstration can be recorded with provenance
Supply the two outstanding SCE performance standards and the verifier findings against them

sections.text: This is decision-support that PRECEDES competent-authority acceptance. It does not assert, and must not be read as, an accepted/approved/authorised safety case. A competent person must review it; acceptance is the act of HSE + OPRED.

items: Offshore Installations (Offshore Safety Directive) (Safety Case etc.) Regulations 2015 — SI 2015/398 (current; SCR 2005 is the superseded legacy reference)
ALARP demonstration principles (external QRA recorded, never computed here)
